_INSTRUCTIONS: Define your supply chain business rules here. These policies tell Hermes when to reorder, how much safety stock to hold, and what your budget is.

safety_stock._comment: Safety stock = the minimum amount of supply you always want to have on hand (a buffer against uncertainty). Measured in weeks of supply.
safety_stock.default_minimum_weeks: TODO — e.g. 2 (keep at least 2 weeks of supply at all times)

reorder_rules._comment: The reorder point is the trigger level — when stock falls to this many weeks of supply, the system recommends an order. Should be higher than safety_stock to allow time for delivery.
reorder_rules.reorder_point_weeks: TODO — e.g. 8 (order when 8 weeks of supply remain)
reorder_rules._comment2: How much to order each time: minimum and maximum number of packs/units per order.

maximum_stock_levels._comment: The ceiling — the system will not recommend building stock above this level. Prevents over-stocking and shelf-life risk.
maximum_stock_levels.default_maximum_weeks: TODO — e.g. 12 (never hold more than 12 weeks of supply at any location)

budget._comment: Total supply chain budget for this study. Set budget_envelope to null if budget tracking is not needed.

expiry_rules._comment: Minimum shelf life remaining at the time a shipment is sent. If a lot has less than this, it should not be shipped.
expiry_rules.minimum_remaining_shelf_life_months: TODO — e.g. 6 (items must have at least 6 months remaining at time of shipment)

sop_references._comment: List the Standard Operating Procedures that govern supply chain activity for this study
sop_references.description: TODO — e.g. Governs the process for placing supply orders
